Explanation: DIFFERENTIATION and MODIFICATION are overarching terms that can represent both adjustments and accommodations in teaching and learning.

Modification: An accommodation allows a student to work AT LEVEL in ways that may not be conventional or 'usual' but support the student's specific needs as they access the curriculum. Accommodations may be academic, but they also may be environmental or behavioural. Also known as 'adaptions'.
Explanation: Definition - accommodation keeps student at curriculum level

Modification: An adjustment inherently changes the task to meet a student at their point of need when they are unable to work at level. Also known as 'modifications'
Explanation: Definition - adjustment modifies curriculum level expectations

Modification: Using a graphic organiser before writing to support organisation
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Only using the graphic organiser and not submitting the 'writing piece'
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Typing on a laptop instead of handwriting when spelling, grammar, handwriting ARE NOT being assessed (or these functions are turned off)
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Typing on a laptop instead of handwriting when spelling, grammar, handwriting ARE being assessed, and these functions are available
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Using a scribe who writes purely as directed by the student when spelling and handwriting ARE NOT being assessed
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Using a scribe who makes suggestions, infers meaning, edits for/corrects grammar, tense and other features
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Responding to questions verbally to demonstrate understanding where writing processes ARE NOT being assessed (e.g. I can verbally answer a question about how the water cycle works if the teacher wants to know if I have understood it)
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Responding to questions verbally where writing processes ARE being assessed (e.g. I can answer verbally to show that I know the content but what is actually being assessed is my ability to write a well structured paragraph on the topic)
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Checklists and sequencing visuals to support general organisation of materials, information or task
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Using checklists or sequencing visuals when knowing / remembering the sequence is what is being assessed.
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Extra time to complete tasks where processing speed is a concern or time is taken by other accommodations such as movement breaks
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Extra time given where there is no other accommodation, where processing and working speed is not a concern and the task is timed for all students
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Providing vocabulary lists / glossary as a reference during learning
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Providing vocabulary lists / glossary as a reference during assessment where use of technical terms is being assessed
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Alternative communication - Braille, Sign Language, second language translation etc
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Screen readers (C-Pen, text to talk software) and audio texts (Audible, audio books etc) or being read to where the skill of reading IS NOT being assessed
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Screen readers, audio texts and being read to where the skill of reading IS being assessed
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Class content posted in LMS (Learning Management System - eg Compass, Google Classroom, Seqta, Toddle) to support prior and post-lesson access
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Alternate assessment options where the mode of assessment DOES NOT form part of the assessment e.g. Telling and pointing to the parts of a flower rather than writing labels on a picture
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Alternate assessment options where the mode of assessment DOES form part of the assessment e.g. writing a speech rather than completing an oral presentation where tone, pace, volume etc are being assessed
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Breaking presentation of class materials or tasks into smaller (more manageable / less overwhelming) sections - known as 'chunking'
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Simplifying class materials or tasks to facilitate 'point of need' teaching and learning (lower order thinking skills)
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Using reverse thinking chains to scaffold steps prior to completing the actual task
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Using reverse thinking chains to change the task
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Lower output expectations where class / curriculum material at level IS STILL BEING addressed
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Lower output expectations resulting in some of the class / curriculum material at level NOT BEING addressed
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Physical support or exemption provided where student is unable to participate - e.g. watch and direct a science experiment where handling chemicals may be impossible or dangerous
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Where physical prowess is assessed such as in Sports and PE an exemption rather than poor performance record is appropriate
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Visually altered class and assessment materials to support visual accessibility - larger font, greyscale images to counteract colour-blindness,  fewer objects / questions per page, use of specific fonts, line spacing considerations, coloured highlights to support dyslexia, specific response structures such tick boxes, lines to write on (or blank page depending on preference)
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Availability of different writing tools - pen, pencil, marker, hand grips, wrist supports etc
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Working in a separate space due to noise or social overwhelm
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Wearing noise cancelling headphones or sound modifying earbuds such as 'Loops' or 'Calmer - Flares'
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Uniform exemption
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Hearing aids, hearing loops etc
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Writing aids such as slanted desks, whiteboards, pencil grips, tablets,
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Speech aids such as communication boards, visual communication cards
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Any form of electronic communication device to counteract disability
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Using a seating plan to accommodate predictability, teacher accessibility or sense of safety in the classroom
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Use of sit/stand desks
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Having a support person close by to provide encouragement, support focus, facilitate movement breaks etc
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Support person does some or all of the work essentially 'for' the student without student understanding or direction.
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Use of visual communication tools to seek support
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Alternate seating arrangements (eg working on the floor or sitting in a chair whilst others are on the floor)
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Eating / drinking during class
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Gum or sensory chew tools
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Wobble cushions / stools / chairs
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Brain breaks or movement breaks
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Use of fidget tools
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Group tasks to support processing via conversation where independent ideas ARE NOT being assessed
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Group tasks to support processing via conversation where independent ideas ARE being assessed may require specific tools to record individual contributions
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations

Modification: Scheduling to prioritise specific classes or times of day where a student cannot manage school on a full timetable
Explanation: Accommodation - student works at grade level with support

Modification: Modified timetable rather than recorded absences are appropriate.
Explanation: Adjustment - student works below grade level expectations
